Incident_Narrative: Upon arrival pt is a 34 yo m found supine on the ground with cpr being performed. Bystander cpr was discontinued once crew reached pts side. Pt was breathing on his own and pt became alert and oriented. Per bystanders they gave pt 8mg of narcan IN prior to ems arrival. Pt admits to snorting fentanyl this afternoon. Pt denies any other drug or etoh use. Pt sts he currently ''doesn't feel right.'' Pt sts he feels hot and cold, but otherwise he cannot clarify his complaints. Pt admits to overdosing on fentanyl in the past. No changes during care. Pt moved to bed @ West ER. Transfer of care RN.

Arrived to find E-14 with patient. Family at residence as well. Patient has been administered Narcan 4mg intra nasal. We understand that the patient is known to use drugs recreationally and was found by family to be unresponsive on floor of kitchen. 
Patient presents, on first contact, unconscious and responsive to pain. Pupils are constricted and reactive. Patient moved to cot via man-sac.
In transport, patient awoke to be AAOX4, pupils remained constricted and reactive. +ROMX4, BD soft non-distended, PW&D. Patient first denies any recreational drug use but soon admits to using and is remorseful for the situation. Patient removed o2 and maintained a good room air sat.
Transport POC without incident. All technique aseptic. Report to ED before and after transport.

C: Dispatched emergency traffic to unknown problem man down. Crew responded immediate to scene responding from station to scene. While crew responded to scene no further information available on pt condition or status. 

H: Pt is a 31 year old male found unresponsive on ground in front parking lot of store. Pt possible taken unknown substance at this time. Pt has no one around other than caller that doesn't know pt. Pt given Narcan at this time and pt has awoken and is alert at this time. 

A: Noted.

R: Pt direct lifted from ground and placed onto awaiting cot. Pt given Narcan at this time and pt has awoken. Pt is alert and awake and able to answer all questions at this time. Pt refusing transport at this time. Pt advised by Engine 37 crew and Medic crew that Narcan could stop working and pt could go back into a overdose state which could result in his death. Pt understood risks of not being transport. Pt nderstood risks of not being transported and signed refusal. 

T: No transport at this time. Pt signed refusal witness by Officer on scene. District 15 contacted and advised of refusal. Crew left care of pt in officers at this time. Pt advised to call 911 back if he changed his mind on being transported to ER. Pt understood that he could call 911 back.

INITIAL CALL CAME THROUGH FOR CARDIAC ARREST. THE CALL WAS CHANGED, WHILE MED21 WAS ENROUTE, TO OVERDOSE. ACCORDING TO MNPD OFFICERS, THEY ADMINISTERED 8MG NARCAN PRIOR TO EMS ARRIVAL. UPON EMS ARRIVAL, THE PT WAS WALKING OUT TO THE AMBULANCE STATING HE DID NOT WANT TO GO TO THE HOSPITAL. THE PT WALKED INSIDE THE AMBULANCE AND THEN OPENED THE BACK DOOR AND RAN OUT, RUNNING DOWN THE STREET. PT RAN OFF FROM SCENE STATING HE WANTED NO HELP. DISTRICT 20 NOTIFIED. MED 21 RETURNED TO SERVICE.

MED 21 ARRIVED ON SCENE WITH ENGINE 21 TO FIND A 35 Y/O MALE LAYING SUPINE ON THE GROUND AT A HOMELESS CAMPSITE, UNRESPONSIVE WITH AGONAL RESPIRATIONS AND PINPOINT PUPILS. ACCORDING TO THE BYSTANDER, WHO CALLED 9-1-1, HE PERFORMED CPR ON THE PT FOR APPROXIMATELY 20 MINUTES PRIOR TO STOPPING AND WALKING ACROSS THE STREET TO A PAY PHONE, WHERE HE CALLED FOR HELP. THE BYSTANDER STATED HE GAVE 2 IM DOSES OF 0.4MG NARCAN PRIOR TO EMS ARRIVAL. THE BYSTANDER ALSO STATED THE PT HAD TAKEN HEROINE AND POSSIBLY METH. THE BYSTANDER DID NOT KNOW ANY INFORMATION ABOUT THE PT OTHER THAN HIS FIRST NAME IS JOSHUA, AND HE WAS 35 Y/O. 

UPON EMS ARRIVAL, THE PT WAS ASSESSED. A PULSE AND RESPIRATIONS WERE NOTED, SO CPR WAS WITHHELD. NFD ATTEMPTED TO PICK THE PT UP AND PLACE HIM ON A MANSAC, BUT THE PT SAT UP. THE PT WAS ASSISTED TO HIS FEET AND HELPED OVER TO THE STRETCHER WITH 5 STRAPS FASTENED AND 2 RAILS UP. A WET/RASPY LUNG SOUND WAS HEARD AT THAT TIME WHEN THE PT BREATHED. THE PT WAS PLACED IN AMBULANCE FOR VITALS AND FURTHER TREATMENT. ALL WERE NOTED. AFTER BASELINE VITALS, THE PT WAS PLACED ON MONITOR, PER PROTOCOL. THE PT WAS ADMINISTERED OXYGEN VIA NASAL CANULA @ 4LPM. EMS ESTABLISHED AN IV. THE PT RESPIRATORY DRIVE STAYED WITHIN NORMAL LIMITS AT THAT TIME. THE PT WAS HARD TO AROUSE, AND HE WAS NOT ABLE TO ANSWER ANY QUESTIONS. AFTER A FEW MINUTES, THE PT SATS DID NOT IMPROVE TO DESIRED EFFECT, SO THE PT WAS PLACED ON NRB MASK @ 15LPM. THE PT SATS IMPROVED SHORTLY AFTER, BUT THE PT RESPIRATORY DRIVE DECREASED, SO EMS ADMINISTERED 0.5MG NARCAN IV. THE PT TOLERATED THE TREATMENT AND RESPIRATORY DRIVE IMPROVED AS WELL AS SATS.. A BG WAS OBTAINED WITH A SERUM GLUCOSE OF 395.  NO OTHER TREATMENTS WERE PERFORMED DUE TO PT REMAINING IN ALTERED MENTAL STATUS.

THE PT WAS CLOSELY MONITORED ENROUTE NON-EMERGENCY TRAFFIC TO GENERAL HOSPITAL. PT VITALS AND CONDITION IMPROVED DURING TRANSPORT BUT REMAINED ALTERED. PT CARE WAS TRANSFERRED OVER TO ER STAFF IN ROOM 18 AFTER FULL REPORT WAS GIVEN. MED 21 RETURNED TO SERVICE.

Upon arrival pt is a 34 yo m found supine on the ground with cpr being performed. Bystander cpr was discontinued once crew reached pts side. Pt was breathing on his own and pt became alert and oriented. Per bystanders they gave pt 8mg of narcan IN prior to ems arrival. Pt admits to snorting fentanyl this afternoon. Pt denies any other drug or etoh use. Pt sts he currently ''doesn't feel right.'' Pt sts he feels hot and cold, but otherwise he cannot clarify his complaints. Pt admits to overdosing on fentanyl in the past. No changes during care. Pt moved to bed @ West ER. Transfer of care RN.

Pt was breathing on his own and pt became alert and oriented. Per bystanders they gave pt 8mg of narcan IN prior to ems arrival. Pt admits to snorting fentanyl this afternoon. Pt denies any other drug or etoh use. Pt sts he currently ''doesn't feel right.'' Pt sts he feels hot and cold, but otherwise he cannot clarify his complaints. Medic 9 dispatched emergency traffic to 110 3rd Ave S, The Moxy Hotel, for cardiac arrest. Upon ems arrival 30 year old male found lying supine on the floor of the hotel. He has a pulse and is breathing slow irregular respirations. He was already administered 2mg Of Narcan IN by first responders prior to our arrival. His wife is on scene and states he went downstairs and came back to the hotel room, went to the bathroom and then came out and went unresponsive. There is a straw found in the bathroom by PD. The patient has pinpoint pupils. His respirations have not improved with the first dose of Narcan. Ventilations are assisted with a BVM with O2 @ 15 LPM. Another 2mg of Narcan is given IN. The patient is still not waking up but his respirations are improving. Pt secured on cot and cot secured in truck. Pt vitals obtained. Pt placed on cardiac monitor- sinus tach. 20 g iv established in pt right ac with Lactated ringers running kvo. NPA is placed in pt right nostril. He is placed on O2 @ 15LPM via NRB Mask. Pt is administered another .5 MG of Narcan via IV. His O2 saturation and respirations are now adequate and being maintained without assistance. Pt transported emergency traffic to St Thomas Midtown Er without incident. Report called into er. Upon arrival to Midtown pt taken to a hall bed. Pt care and verbal report left with RN and MD. 

End Report.

30s male had taken some heroin and passed out and went unresponsive per friend. He had some Narcan on him and gave it IN just prior to my arrival.  pt presents standing up a little confused initially but fully a,a,ox3 after a minute or so. has a hx of MS but no other issues other than occasional drug use.  a-paten, b-bs=cb, c-strong pulses. Offered transport but he refused. Risks explained and understood. Friend will be w him and call back if necessary. Refusal obtained and report given to Dist 5.  10-8

Arrived on scene and found a 35 y/o female standing on along a roadway near a homeless camp. Patient awake, alert and oriented. Chief complaint overdose. Patient helped to and secured to stretcher in ems unit. Patient advised she snorted heroin prior to our arrival. Bystanders report they administered narcan after she became unresponsive. Head to toe assessment completed. Patient complains of feeling cold. Patient denies pain. Patient has clear and equal bilateral breath sounds. Vitals assessed. Patient placed on monitor. Patient transported non emergency to Centennial. Enroute vitals monitored. Patient remained awake and alert throughout transport. Patient warmed with blanket. Radio report called and acknowledged by receiving facility. Arrived at Centennial with no further changes. Patient care and report transferred to ER nursing staff.

UOA we found a 38 YO M being brought out to the ambulance by Mission  staff. PT was A&Ox4. Employees stated they found him in the bathroom stall not breathing. They did not check for a pulse. They did 2 rounds of 30 chest compressions and gave 3 doses of 4 mg prior to our arrival. They stated PT woke up shortly after giving the 3rd dose of Narcan. PT seemed slightly lethargic. He denied any other complaints. He admitted to using fentanyl. PT had no changes during transport.

MED 09 ARRIVED TO FIND 52 YO MALE DOWN ON THE GROUND. PT ADMIN NARCAN PRIOR TO EMS ARRIVAL. PT AWOKE UPON BEING LIFTED OFF THE GROUND. PT STATES HE DOESNT WANT TO GO TO THE HOSPITAL. PT DENIES TAKING ANY NARCOTICS. PT STATES HE HAS JUST BEEN DRINKING. PT DENIES ANY COMPLAINTS. PT ADVISED OF MULTITUDE OF RISKS OF REFUSAL INCLUDING DEATH. PT VITALS OBTAINED. PT AGAIN STATES HE DOESNT WISH TO GO TO THE HOSPITAL. PT STATES HE IS GOING TO WALK HOME WITH HIS FRIENDS. PT ADVISED TO CALL BACK AT ANY TIME. PT LEFT ON SCENE WITH FRIENDS. NO FURTHER PT CONTACT.

UOA we found Staff members standing around a 28 YO M lying supine on the ground outside of the Mission. Staff members stated he was given a total of 8 mg of Narcan IN. The did not comment when asked if PT was breathing. Upon PT contact PT was A&Ox4. He seemed lethargic but was A&Ox4. He would not allow us to get VS, check BG and put him on the EKG monitor. PT had no changes during transport. 

All VS were gathered from MidTown ER.

EMS dispatched for a man down. Upon arrival Pt. is seen lying supine on the sidewalk and is not responding to verbal stimuli. Nashville Fire Dept. Firefighters administered Narcan IN as noted prior to EMS arrival. There was never a loss of pulse nor CPR performed.

Upon assessment, Pt. has minimal response to painful stimuli. Pt. is not currently maintaining his airway on his own. Pt. has agonal breathing at a slow rate. Skin color is pale and diaphoretic. Pupils were pinpoint and fixed. Pt. was moved to the cot and secured with straps x5 with rails up. Pt. was placed in upright position and placed on a NRB. Initial vitals were taken as noted with no abnormalities noted. Pt. was placed on a cardiac monitor showing sinus tachycardia. Pt. began to become more alert. As EMS was trying to obtain IV access, Pt. became fully alert. Due to Pt. movement and onset of diaphoresis, EMS was unable to keep cardiac monitor on the patient. Pt. admitted to using ''Ice'' regularly. Pt. states ''Someone gave me a can of crack for free and that's what I used''. Pt. was monitored closely for mental status changes throughout transport. Pt. was transported non emergent. There were no further interventions indicated. No changes during transport. Report was given to the attending nurse and Pt. was left in their care. EMS cleared the destination and returned to service.

EMS dispatched to scene. Upon arrival found Pt ambulatory on scene, alert, oriented, and able to answer questions appropriately. Nurses on scene states that they had given a total of 16 mg of Narcan before the Pt was responsive. Staff states Pt was unresponsive for about 5 min and was turning blue. Pt states that he had not taken any narcotics. Pt states that he was just tired. Pt at this time has no complaints. Pt self ambulated onto stretcher. pt was secured to stretcher, Pt taken to ambulance, Pt reassessed, vitals obtained, transport initiated to General ER. WHil in route to ER, Pt had no change in condition. Pt taken to ER bed and moved over via self ambulate. Pt care report given and nurse signed for Pt care.

Arrived on scene and found a 45 y/o male sitting in the lobby at the mission. Patient awake, alert and oriented. Chief complaint overdose. Patient advised he smoked fentanyl in a joint. Patient advised staff he was not feeling well. Staff administered Narcan and called for ems. Head to toe assessment completed. Patient complains of dizziness and feeling unwell. Patient denies pain. Patient has clear and equal bilateral breath sounds. Patient helped to and secured to stretcher. Patient placed in ems unit. Vitals assessed. Patient placed on monitor. 12-lead EKG acquired. Patient transported non emergency to Centennial. Enroute vitals monitored. Patient remained awake and alert throughout transport. Radio report called and acknowledged by receiving facility. Arrived at Centennial with no further changes. Patient care and report transferred to ER nursing staff.

Unit responded to the previously stated location for an OVERDOSE. Upon our arrival the Pt .was lying on the ground. PD had given narcan IN prior to our arrival. Pt. was having snoring respirations and unresponsive. Pt. was lifted and placed on the cot, secured via all straps and rails and initial vitals were obtained. Pt. was given IM narcan, Pt. status improved. Pt. woke up. Unit began transport non to the ER.
Pt. was AAOx3 after narcan, had a patent airway, normal respirations, NO sign of trauma noted. NO fluids noted coming from the facial region. Pupils were PEARLA approx 4mm in size. Trachea was midline. Equal bilateral chest rise and fall with clear breath sounds noted. Abdomen was soft non tender. pelvis was stable. Positive PMS in all extremities, Pt. status continued to improve during transport.
Pt. care report was given, NO questions asked,.
Bedside report was given, NO questions asked.
Care given to nurse. unit returned to service.

Paramedic CC Scharlau Dylan 210857

In summary, Medic 19 dispatched to street address for overdose. Upon arrival, patient found sitting in the back seat of a taxi, alert but confused, and making sudden muscle movements with no clear intention. Crew noted the patient was not answering questions but was muttering random nonsensical words to herself. Engine 19 crew stated they administered narcan prior to EMS arrival. Patient lifted to stretcher where she was secured and loaded into the ambulance. Vitals and further assessment performed. Crew noted no abnormalities of patient's lung sounds or skin parameters. 12-lead ECG acquired and transmitted. Full assessment not performed due to patient being unable to answer questions. BGL of 88 noted. Patient transported non-emergent to CMC ER with no decline in condition noted. Vitals reassessed en route. Report given via radio with no orders received. At destination, patient transferred to hospital bed in ER room 14. Report given to RN. All patient care handed over to hospital staff. EOR.

Upon arrival pt is a 63 yo f found slumped over in bed, initially awake but only moaning, abc-intact w/a cc of AMS. Both of pts son's were on scene. Family sts for the past hour pt has been slumped in bed, moaning, and not responding to them. Family sts pt has been sick with cough and congestion. Family pt also has a hx of heroin abuse. Family sts they have pt 4mg IN narcan prior to ems arrival. While I was reviewing pts pmhx with family, pt became alert and oriented x4. Pt adamantly refuses transport. Pt admits to using opiates this AM. I informed pt if the narcan wears off before the opiates, then she can go unresponsive, and stop breathing. Pts spo2 was 86% RA and pts HR was 128bpm. I informed pt her spo2 is low and she needs to be on o2. I also informed pt since her spo2 is low, and she has cough/congestion, she needs a chest xray to r/o lower respiratory infection. I advised pt of the risks associated with refusing ems care, up to, and including death. Pt understood the risks and still refused. Pt signed refusal with her son witnessing. I told the pt and family to call back if she decides she wants transport, or if she gets altered again. As I was leaving I told family to try to convince pt to go to the hospital pov. D38 notified.

Medic 19 dispatched to 11th Ave S at McGavock St for an overdose.  Upon arrival found a 27 year old black male lying supine on the ground.  As EMS pulled up CPR was being performed by bystanders.  Patient was found unresponsive by bystanders and then found to be apneic and pulseless so they began CPR.  Squad 9 reports that they felt a pulse, CPR stopped.  Patient's pupils are pinpoint and his skin is ashen with signs of cyanosis but dry and warm.  Patient has agonal respirations.  Administered 2mg of Narcan IN.  Placed a 30FR NPA in the patient's right nare.  Began PPV via BVM.  Patient placed on cardiac monitor, patient in sinus tachycardia.  Obtained patient's vitals.  Administered 2mg of Narcan IN.  Patient lifted to the cot by crew, secured with straps and rails, cot to unit secured.  Patient's breathing improved to an adequate rate and depth.  PPV stopped and patient placed on O2 via NRB at 15 L/Min.  Established 18g IV in patient's left wrist, first attempt successful.  Began emergency transport to Centennial ER.  Continued to monitor patient's status and vitals.  Patient began waking up and becoming more alert.  Called into ER with patient report and ETA.  Arrived at ER without incident.  Patient is now fully responsive and able to answer questions.  Patient admits to using fentanyl.  Patient taken to ER bed 8.  Patient care and report given to on duty nurse, signature obtained.  Medic 19 clear of ER.

Medic 06 dispatched emergency traffic to the address listed for a 47yo M chief complaint of syncople episode. Upon arrival to facility met with nurse on staff and pt. Pt was AAOx4 and ambulatory without assistance. Pt appeared in no obvious distress. Pt walked without assistance to the stretcher and was secured. Nursing staff noted a total loss of consciousness while pt was in bed and that he had received the  Narcan noted in the medications tab. Pt at this time had no complaint. Pt was taken to the ambulance.

Once in the ambulance pt vitals taken at this time and monitored throughout transport. Pt assessment performed at this time. Pt denied any trauma and noted he was laying in bed. Pt noted no chest pain or shortness of breath. Pt denied any head neck or back pain. Pt denied any drugs or alcohol. Pt was transported non-emergency to General. Pt radio report called in to receiving facility. Pt noted a headache that developed during transport. No other changes noted during transport. No other interventions performed throughout transport. 

Upon arrival to general pt was taken to bed 4 and transferred himself to the bed. Pt report given to nursing staff and care transferred to facility. All signatures obtained and documentation completed. Medic 06 returned to service.

MEDIC 15 DISPATCHED TO APARTMENT FOR 25 YO/M THAT TOOK UNKNOWN DRUGS AND STARTED YELLING OUT AND HITTING AND SCRATCHING HIMSELF. PTS MOTHER CALLED 911. PRIOR TO ARRIVAL, ENG 26 REQUESTED PD HOT TO SCENE DUE TO PTS VIOLENT NATURE. PRIOR TO ARRIVAL, PTS MOTHER GAVE HIM 4MG NARCAN INTRANASAL AND PT STARTED CALMING DOWN. AOS TO FIND PT LYING IN BED, AAOX4, ABCX3, PMSX4. PT SLAPPED HIMSELF ABOUT 2-3 MORE TIMES AND THEN CALMED DOWN. PT INITIALLY WANTED TO REFUSE TRANSPORT BUT WAS CONVINCED TO BE EVALUATED AT THE HOSPITAL. PT HAD NO OTHER OUTBURSTS WITH NFD AT THIS TIME. PT WALKED TO STRETCHER, SEMIFOWLER FOR TRANSPORT, SECURED WITH STRAPS AND RAILS. MOVED PT TO AMBULANCE. TRANSPORTED NON EMERGENT TO SUMMIT ER. VITALS OBTIANED AND DOCUMENTED IN REPORT, SINUS TACH NO ECTOPY, BGL DOCUMENTED, SKIN PWD, PERRL, HEENT NORMAL, NO JVD, TRACH MIDLINE, BS-CEBL ABD SOFT NON TENDER, NO EDEMA, PT HAS NO OTHER COMPLAINTS. PT HAS MILD ABRASIONS AND SCRATCHES TO HIS CHEST AND HAND PRINTS ON HIS FACE FROM SLAPPING HIMSELF, NO OTHER INJURIES NOTED. NO OTHER CHANGES ENROUTE. MOVED PT TO ER BED. GAVE REPORT AND RELEASED PT CARE TO NURSE. MEDIC 15 CLEAR AND BACK INSERVICE. PVD

Medic 9 dispatched emergency traffic to 26 Green St. Upon our arrival 31 year old female found lying supine in a drive way. Bystanders were doing chest compressions when we arrived. Its reported she was found unconscious not breathing. She was administered 4mg of Narcan IN prior to our arrival. She is breathing with slow irregular respirations and she has a strong bounding pulse. She has pinpoint pupils. Pt secured on cot and cot secured in truck. Pt is administered another 1mg of Narcan IN. NPA is placed in pt left nostril and she is placed on O2 @ 15lpm via NRB. Pt vitals obtained. Pt placed on cardiac monitor- sinus arrhythmia. Pt is now breathing regularly. Pt assessment performed cebbs, pinpoint pupils, no jvd, heent normal, skin pwd. Pt began to vomit. The NRB was removed and her airway is suctioned, cleared and NRB replaced. Pt began moaning towards the end of transport but never woke up. Pt transported emergency traffic to Centennial Medical Center ER without incident. Report called into er. Upon arrival to Centennial pt taken to room 7. Pt care and verbal report left with RN and MD. 

End Report.

Medic 8 dispatched for overdose. 
Upon arrival EMS locates a 27 year old female, laying in the fetal position, moaning on the floor. Bystanders report the patient was found unresponsive, pulseless and apneic. CPR was performed. Bystanders state this location is a halfway house and the patient was just released from jail. 16mg Narcan was administered IN prior to EMS arrival.
Patient is assisted to her feet and assisted downstairs to awaiting cot. Patient is seated semi-fowlers with all belts secured and rails in the upright and locked position. 
Paramedic assessment completed. Patient is alert to verbal stimuli and confused. Patient's airway is open and patent, equal chest rise/fall noted. PMS intact x4. Skin pink/cool/dry. Pupils 3mm. Vital signs obtained on scene, sinus tachycardia noted. 
Patient presents in moderate distress due to feeling cold. Patient reports her chest hurts. Patient in uncooperative with remaining in the seated position on stretcher, removing seat belts and rolling into face down fetal position. 
Patient's vital signs are monitored enroute to Vanderbilt ED with no critical variations to report. Sinus tachycardia continues.
Upon arrival patient is placed in ED bed A13. 
Patient care report is given and transfer of care signature is obtained.
Medic 8 clear.

Dispatched to unresponsive. Medic 19 aos to find aa m, unresponsive on the floor in the kitchen, strong pulse, breathing 6-10 times per min, skin warm,dry, with good skin tone, with a cc from his brother that he smoked something and then hit the floor. Pt was given 2mg narcan IN, and responded 30 seconds to 1 minute later. Pt was then placed on stretcher, secured, and then loaded and secured into the unit. Vitals obtained and recorded. IV successfully started with no complications. Pt said he had no hx or ax. Pt was then transported while being continuously monitored en route. En route, pt remained awake, alert, good chest rise and fall, breathing 12-16 times per min, with no new onset of signs or symptoms. Pt arrived at ED, where pt care report transferred face to face, staff signed ticket, and Medic 19 returned to service.

En40 arrive on scene with Medic40 to find pt sitting in vehicle unresponsive to verbal or physical prompts , leaning over the steering wheel. Pt was administered 4mg of Narcan intranasal and assisted with breathing with BVM. Pt was secured to the stretcher with straps and inside medic unit for vitals and transport. FF crew members assisted medic during transport en route to hospital. Upon arrival at the hospital, Pt was received by ER staff. EOR

M09 was dispatched for a 33 YOF at a homeless camp with a chief complaint of being lethargic and sick. We arrived and found the patient outside in a wheel chair. She was slow to answer questions and unable to follow commands. Patients friend told us that the patient had been missing for 3 days and was found in this state. Patient had drug paraphenila on her and stated that she had used heroin about 6 hours prior. We lifted the patient onto the stretcher, secured her, and loaded her into the unit. Patients vitals were assessed and warming measures were used as she was cold. No IV site found. Patient not given narcan as she was breathing normally and sats were good. 3 lead placed and we proceeded to transport the patient to the ED and monitored vitals during transport. Upon arrival the patient was registered and brought to an exam room. We were met by nursing staff and gave a patient care report. Patient care was transferred to nursing staff. Patient unable to sign due to her AMS. Patient was homeless and had no address.

Medic 41 responded immediately to walk up at station 41. The pt is a passenger in a truck that reported the pt started falling asleep and would not wake up. The pt is showing obvious signs of opioid overdose with slow irregular nd snoring respiration with pinpoint pupils and reported recovering addict. The pt was given oxygen via BVM. Pt was unresponsive with IV established and Narcan given. The pt improved with the Narcan with respiration improving and the pt waking becoming confused and agitated. The pt was calmed when vitals and assessment was acquired and noted. The pt was advised to allow transport to the ED for evaluation. The pt denied taking any substances and refused transport to the ED. The pt was advised of the risks of relapsing and overdosing again if the Narcan wears off and opioids are still in his system. The pt again denied any opioids or anything else and stated he was fine and did not want to go to the ED. The pt was AOx4 and answering all questions and following commands when making his decision with risks given. District 15 was notified and refusal obtained and witnessed. Leave behind overdose kit was left with the pt and the pt friend advised he would watch him and call back if anything changed. Medic 41 returned to service.

Disp for 32 y/o Female possible overdose. AOS with lights and sirens. ENg 10 on scene prior to EMS. Eng 10 reported that patient was found in passenger seat of parked vehicle. Patient's mother reported that patient has a history of Cocaine and Xanax use. Mother explained that patient was unresponsive, so Narcan was given IM. Mother reported that she gave 8 mg of Narcan total. Patient was found awake and alert in vehicle. Patient was emotional and yelling, ''I'm sorry''. Patient was calmed and admitted to using Cocaine and Xanax prior to overdosing. Patient had noticeable dry blood around nose. Mother stated that the blood appeared when administering Narcan. All bleeding had stopped prior to EMS arrival. Patient was placed on main stretcher and secured for transport. Vital signs were obtained and documented. EKG monitored. During transport, patient remained awake, with no changes in health. Patient remained A&Ox4/4. Southern Hills ER was given full oral report. Patient was placed in ER room 13 under nursing staff care. Narcan kit was left in patients room with patient and patient's mother.

Medic 3 dispatched to the listed location for Unconscious Pt/Overdose. U/O/A found approx 25 y/o/f supine in bed unresponsive with agonal respirations and pinpoint pupils. A- compromised B- agonal C- + regular radial pulse, skin- pink, warm and dry D- unresponsive for approx 30 minutes. Bystanders deny knowing pt name or her Hx and that they found pt unresponsive. Pt given Narcan as noted. Do crowd placed pt on pt mover and carried outside and secured on cot and placed in ambulance. Assessment as noted. Assisted ventilations via BVM and 26FR OPA placed with 100% o2. VS as noted and pt placed on monitor. Pt resp rate increased and now grunting and still unresponsive. IV established as noted. Pt resp improved and pt placed on o2 via NRB with NPA still in place. Pt transported emergency to the ER. Pt given second dose of Narcan as noted. Pt became awake and agitated with screaming and unable to understand what pt was saying. . Pt attempting to get off cot and took off o2. Pt at the ED placed in ER bed and report given and pt left in care of the ED.
